spec_match.rationale: Builds an Attribution-Reporting-Register-Trigger payload with arbitrary length keys exactly as the spec change permits; aggregatable_values keys correctly mirror the source_keys.

interactive.rationale: Add key / add a 200-char key / reset buttons plus per-key input fields drive a live JSON view — real composition, not a styled code card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature card promises live JSON output for copy-paste with descriptive key names — exactly what the page does.

edge_cases.rationale: Verdict pill always emits 'valid (v131 trigger: any length keys)' — never demonstrates the validation that DOES still apply (duplicate key names within one entry, missing key_piece, invalid hex). Also never demonstrates the new aggregatable values per-key 32-bit cap.

design_system.rationale: Mostly uses CSS variables, but the dynamically created input/button elements inline raw hex (`#000`, `#fff`) instead of using CSS variables — the page should hit `--bg-paper`/`--border-black`/`--text-black` like the rest of the page.

openQuestions.title: Replace inline raw hex with CSS variables
openQuestions.detail: The dynamically created input rows use `#000` and `#fff` inline. Per design system rules, switch to `var(--border-black)` and `var(--bg-paper)`. Otherwise these elements won't track theme changes.

openQuestions.title: Surface the validations that ARE still in place
openQuestions.detail: Right now the verdict always says 'valid'. Add a duplicate-key check, an empty-key check, and visually flag when total aggregatable_values exceed L1 contribution budget. This makes 'no length cap' more honest by showing what's still being validated.

summary: Resolved the required ChromeStatus reference; 2 follow-up question(s) remain.
